emails.subject: Missing Mill Test Report — PO #MER-PO-107294 — 04/14/2026
emails.body_text:
Hi Susan,

I'm writing regarding the shipment received today at our Meridian, MS plant under PO #MER-PO-107294 from Consolidated Forge Works.

The shipment of 200 units of FG-91144-A1 (Forged gear blank) arrived with the Certificate of Conformance (COC) but without the required Mill Test Report (MTR). As FG-91144-A1 is a safety-critical part, the MTR is a mandatory document per our receiving requirements. Without it, we cannot verify the heat numbers (H-8827-A, H-8827-B listed on the COC) against the steel mill's chemical and mechanical property certifications.

The material has been received into our facility but has been placed in quarantine (Q-HOLD) pending receipt of the MTR. It cannot be released to production until the MTR is received and verified.

Please send the MTR for heat numbers H-8827-A and H-8827-B as a PDF attachment to this email address at your earliest opportunity. If we do not receive the MTR within 5 business days (by 04/21/2026), we will escalate per our internal procedures.

Best regards,
Inbound Materials Coordinator
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant
receiving.meridian@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: NCR-MER-2026-0251 | Major | Consolidated Forge Works | MER-PO-107294 | Response Required by 04/28/2026
emails.body_text:
Hi Susan,

Please find attached a formal Nonconformance Report (NCR-MER-2026-0251) issued against Consolidated Forge Works in connection with PO #MER-PO-107294.

NCR Details:
- NCR Number: NCR-MER-2026-0251
- Issue Date: 04/14/2026
- Severity: Major
- Type: Documentation (DOC)
- PO Number: MER-PO-107294
- SKU: FG-91144-A1 (Forged gear blank)
- Quantity Affected: 200 units
- Dollar Value: $15,600.00

Description: The shipment was received without the required Mill Test Report (MTR). FG-91144-A1 is a safety-critical forging, and the MTR is mandatory for heat number verification and traceability compliance. The COC was received and lists heat numbers H-8827-A and H-8827-B, but without the MTR, these cannot be verified against the steel mill's certifications. The material has been quarantined pending resolution.

Required Action: Please provide the MTR for heat numbers H-8827-A and H-8827-B and a written corrective action plan describing how this documentation gap will be prevented on future shipments.

Response Required By: 04/28/2026 (10 business days)

Please direct your response to this email address.

Best regards,
Inbound Materials Coordinator
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant
receiving.meridian@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: Critical NCR Draft for Review — NCR-MER-2026-0253, Meridian Steel Supply
emails.body_text:
Theo,

Critical NCR draft for review — NCR-MER-2026-0253, Meridian Steel Supply (SUP-0089), PO #MER-PO-106833.

Brief: Band 3 critical short (-18.8%) on safety-critical AX-04827-B2 axle housing forgings. 78 of 96 units received, 18 units missing. Line-down risk confirmed by Aditi (on-hand 22, consumption 12/day, risk window ~1.8 days). Dollar value: $7,650.00.

PDF at NCRs/NCR-MER-2026-0253_draft.docx. Please review and approve before I send to supplier.

Thanks,
SM

emails.subject: NCR-MER-2026-0253 | Critical | Meridian Steel Supply | MER-PO-106833 | Response Required by 04/17/2026
emails.body_text:
Dear Raj,

Please find attached a formal Nonconformance Report (NCR-MER-2026-0253) issued against Meridian Steel Supply in connection with PO #MER-PO-106833.

This is a CRITICAL severity NCR requiring your immediate attention.

NCR Details:
- NCR Number: NCR-MER-2026-0253
- Issue Date: 04/14/2026
- Severity: Critical
- Type: Quantity (QTY)
- PO Number: MER-PO-106833
- SKU: AX-04827-B2 (Axle housing forging)
- Quantity Ordered: 96 units
- Quantity Received: 78 units
- Quantity Missing: 18 units
- Variance: -18.8% (Band 3 Critical Short)
- Dollar Value: $7,650.00

Description: The shipment was received with a critical quantity shortage of 18 units (-18.8%), classified as Band 3 Critical Short per our receiving procedures. AX-04827-B2 is a safety-critical axle housing forging. Our Production Planning team has confirmed line-down risk within approximately 48 hours based on current on-hand inventory (22 units) and daily consumption rate (12 units/day). The 78 received units have been quarantined pending resolution.

Required Action: Please provide an immediate explanation for the shortage, a confirmed ship date for the 18 missing units, and a written corrective action plan.

Response Required By: 04/17/2026 (3 business days)

Given the production impact, we request your urgent attention to this matter.

Best regards,
Inbound Materials Coordinator
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant
receiving.meridian@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: Critical NCR Draft for Review — NCR-MER-2026-0252, Heartland Bearings LLC
emails.body_text:
Theo,

Critical NCR draft for review — NCR-MER-2026-0252, Heartland Bearings LLC (SUP-0312), PO #MER-PO-108510.

Brief: Tier 2 functional damage on 30 of 144 safety-critical BG-33044-A1 wheel bearings — machined surface scratches confirmed by Reggie Lemoine inspection (posted #quality-alerts 13:20 CST). Supplier on Probation since 03/01/2026. Severity: Critical. Dollar value: $5,565.00 (30 damaged units x $185.50).

Repeat-offender flag posted in #escalations-meridian — this is the 3rd NCR for SUP-0312 in the past 90 days, and a 1st Critical NCR, Probation review recommended.

PDF at NCRs/NCR-MER-2026-0252_draft.docx. Please review and approve before I send to supplier.

Thanks,
SM

emails.subject: RE: Delivery Forecast — May 2026
emails.body_text:
Good morning,

I wanted to touch base regarding next month's delivery forecast. We're currently projecting 3 shipments to Meridian in May:

1. Week of 05/04 — BG-33044-A1, approx. 200 units (against upcoming PO)
2. Week of 05/11 — BG-33044-A1, approx. 150 units
3. Week of 05/18 — SL-20118-C3, approx. 600 units

Could you confirm whether these volumes align with your expected POs? We'd like to coordinate carrier scheduling on our end.

Also, we're planning a brief maintenance shutdown of our grinding line 05/25–05/30. This should not affect any open orders but I wanted to flag it proactively.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Doug Linden
Commercial Account Manager
Heartland Bearings LLC
dlinden@heartlandbearings.com

emails.subject: Re: Missing Mill Test Report — PO #MER-PO-107294 — 04/14/2026
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Thank you for letting us know. I apologize for the oversight — the MTR for heat numbers H-8827-A and H-8827-B should have been included with the shipment paperwork.

I've reached out to our metallurgical lab and they are pulling the reports now. I expect to have both MTRs in hand and sent to you by close of business today (04/14/2026).

I'll send them as PDF attachments to this email thread as soon as I receive them.

Again, my apologies for the inconvenience.

Best regards,
Susan Park
Quality Assurance Manager
Consolidated Forge Works
spark@conforge.com | (205) 555-0193

emails.subject: Line-Down Risk Assessment — AX-04827-B2, PO #MER-PO-106833
emails.body_text:
SM,

Per your request — line-down risk assessment for AX-04827-B2 (axle housing forging), PO #MER-PO-106833.

Current status:
- On-hand inventory (per inventory_master.xlsx): 22 units
- Daily consumption rate (current production schedule): 12 units/day
- Estimated days of supply: 22 / 12 = approximately 1.8 days
- Risk window: approximately 1.8 days from today, i.e., line-down risk by end of day Thursday 04/16/2026 if no additional supply arrives

Assessment: LINE-DOWN RISK WITHIN 48 HOURS CONFIRMED.

I've checked the production schedule and there is no feasible way to reduce consumption below 10 units/day without halting the medium-duty axle line. We also have no approved substitute SKU for AX-04827-B2.

Next expected delivery for this SKU: PO #MER-PO-106835, scheduled 04/15/2026 (tomorrow), 120 units from Meridian Steel Supply. If this delivery arrives on time and is clean, the line-down risk is resolved. If not, we need to escalate to Plant Manager level for production rescheduling.

I've CC'd Elizabeth Velasquez on this assessment per your escalation.

Let me know if you need anything else.

Aditi Krishnamurthy
Production Planning Lead — Meridian
aditi.krishnamurthy@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: Re: Critical NCR Draft for Review — NCR-MER-2026-0253, Meridian Steel Supply
emails.body_text:
SM,

Reviewed. The NCR-MER-2026-0253 draft looks good. One minor note — please make sure the letter references the specific heat number (H-6203-D) in the description section since this is a safety-critical part and the traceability chain needs to be explicitly documented in the NCR communication.

Otherwise, approved to send.

Theo Brandt
Quality Manager — Meridian Plant
theo.brandt@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: REC-20260414-005 Inspection Report — BG-33044-A1 Tier 2 Damage
emails.body_text:
SM,

Completed inspection of BG-33044-A1 wheel bearing assemblies from REC-20260414-005 (Heartland Bearings LLC, PO #MER-PO-108510, line 001).

Findings:
- Total units inspected: 144
- Units with damage: 30
- Damage type: Machined surface scratches on bearing inner race
- Severity: Tier 2 — Functional damage. The scratches are on load-bearing surfaces and could affect bearing life and performance under load. These units should NOT be released to production without further engineering review.
- Undamaged units (114): Visually acceptable, no defects observed on machined surfaces or outer races
- Root cause assessment: Damage pattern is consistent with inadequate inter-layer dunnage during transit. The scratches are uniform and appear to have been caused by metal-on-metal contact between stacked bearings.

Photos attached: 8 images showing representative damage on affected units, plus 2 images of packaging condition.

Marked-up packing slip attached — I've circled the affected pallet numbers (pallets 2 and 3 of 6).

Recommendation: Quarantine all 144 units pending disposition decision. The 30 damaged units are not suitable for production use in my assessment. The 114 undamaged units may be releasable after Theo's review, but I'd recommend keeping them quarantined as a batch until we hear from Heartland on corrective action.

Posted summary to #quality-alerts as well.

Reggie Lemoine
Quality Inspector — 1st Shift
reggie.lemoine@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: REC-20260414-006 — DS-77201-A3 Putaway Authorization — Theo Brandt Approval
emails.body_text:
SM,

Approved for putaway — REC-20260414-006, DS-77201-A3 driveshaft yoke assembly, 60 units. COC, MTR, and heat number H-5590-B verified on my end. Release to Zone A per standard procedure.

Theo
